criteria.name: Awards
criteria.rationale: A named, competitive prize from a top-tier peer-reviewed venue directly satisfies this criterion.

criteria.name: Membership
criteria.rationale: Reviewer and area-chair roles are scored under Judging; no membership in an association requiring outstanding achievement was mentioned.

criteria.name: Press
criteria.rationale: IEEE Spectrum is a widely-circulated professional publication, but only one outlet is described; additional coverage would solidify this criterion.

criteria.name: Judging
criteria.evidence: Reviewer for JMLR; area chair for NeurIPS
criteria.rationale: Serving as area chair at NeurIPS and peer reviewer for a leading journal demonstrates selection to judge others' work at the highest levels of the field.

criteria.name: Original contribution
criteria.evidence: US patent 12,114,778 for distributed-training method; TENSORLOOM framework (22,000 GitHub stars, adopted at 60+ companies)
criteria.rationale: A granted utility patent plus a widely-adopted open-source artifact with documented industry uptake together constitute original contributions of major significance.

criteria.name: Scholarly articles
criteria.evidence: 27 peer-reviewed papers; 5,100 citations; h-index 38; 2024 ICML oral presentation
criteria.rationale: An h-index of 38 with over 5,000 citations and an oral slot at ICML — a highly selective venue — clearly satisfies this criterion.

criteria.name: Critical role
criteria.evidence: Authored and shipped TENSORLOOM used at 60+ companies; titled Staff Research Scientist
criteria.rationale: Broad industry adoption of a self-authored framework implies a critical role, but the petition would benefit from explicit documentation of a leading or critical position at a named organization of distinguished reputation.

criteria.name: High remuneration
criteria.rationale: $295K is well above median for research scientists, but the record as described lacks an explicit peer-salary benchmark; a wage-survey exhibit (BLS, Levels.fyi, or expert declaration) would confirm this criterion.

gaps: No professional society memberships described — document any IEEE, ACM, or equivalent membership that required peer nomination or evidence of outstanding achievement
Critical role needs an organizational anchor — obtain a letter naming the applicant as critical to a distinguished employer or to TENSORLOOM's adopting companies
Press coverage rests on a single outlet — identify additional media features, interviews, or podcast appearances in mainstream or professional press
High remuneration requires a benchmarking exhibit — prepare a wage-survey or expert declaration comparing $295K to peers at equivalent experience levels
Membership criterion is unaddressed — explore whether any fellowship, distinguished-member grade, or invited society membership can be documented
disclaimer: This is general informational guidance only, not legal advice. Immigration law is fact-specific and changes frequently. An attorney of record licensed to practice law is required to review your petition and advise on your situation before anything is filed with USCIS.
